text: Those who disbelieve and hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah, He will render their deeds void.
As for those who believe, do good, and have faith in what has been revealed to MuŠł•ammad‚ÄĒwhich is the truth from their Lord‚ÄĒHe will absolve them of their sins and improve their condition.
This is because the disbelievers follow falsehood, while the believers follow the truth from their Lord. This is how Allah shows people their true state ňĻof faithňļ.

So when you meet the disbelievers ňĻin battleňļ, strike ňĻtheirňļ necks until you have thoroughly subdued them, then bind them firmly. Later ňĻfree them either asňļ an act of grace or by ransom until the war comes to an end. So will it be. Had Allah willed, He ňĻHimselfňļ could have inflicted punishment on them. But He does ňĻthis only toňļ test some of you by means of others. And those who are martyred in the cause of Allah, <sup foot_note=1039>1039</sup> He will never render their deeds void.

foot_notes.1039: Another possible interpretation: ‚ÄúAnd those who fight in the cause of Allah.‚ÄĚ

text: He will guide them ňĻto their rewardňļ, improve their condition,
and admit them into Paradise, having made it known to them. <sup foot_note=1040>1040</sup>

foot_notes.1040: Once in Paradise, the believers will recognize their mansions just like they recognized their homes in the world.

text: O believers! If you stand up for Allah, He will help you and make your steps firm.
As for the disbelievers, may they be doomed and may He render their deeds void.
That is because they detest what Allah has revealed, so He has rendered their deeds void.
Have they not travelled throughout the land to see what was the end of those before them? Allah annihilated them, and a similar fate awaits the disbelievers.
This is because Allah is the Patron of the believers while the disbelievers have no patron.

Surely Allah will admit those who believe and do good into Gardens under which rivers flow. As for the disbelievers, they enjoy themselves and feed like cattle. <sup foot_note=1041>1041</sup> But the Fire will be their home.

foot_notes.1041: Meaning, the disbelievers enjoy their life to the fullest while they are heedless of what will happen to them in the Hereafter, just like the cattle that graze in the pasture are unaware that they will probably end up in the slaughter house.

text: ňĻImagine, O Prophet,ňļ how many societies We destroyed that were far superior in might than your society‚ÄĒwhich drove you out‚ÄĒand there was none to help them!
Can those ňĻbelieversňļ who stand on clear proof from their Lord be like those whose evil deeds are made appealing to them and ňĻonlyňļ follow their desires?

The description of the Paradise promised to the righteous is that in it are rivers of fresh water, rivers of milk that never changes in taste, rivers of wine delicious to drink, and rivers of pure honey. There they will ňĻalsoňļ have all kinds of fruit, and forgiveness from their Lord. ňĻCan they beňļ like those who will stay in the Fire forever, left to drink boiling water that will tear apart their insides?

There are some of them who listen to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, but when they depart from you, they say ňĻmockinglyňļ to those ňĻbelieversňļ gifted with knowledge, ‚ÄúWhat did he just say?‚ÄĚ These are the ones whose hearts Allah has sealed and who ňĻonlyňļ follow their desires.
As for those who are ňĻrightlyňļ guided, He increases them in guidance and blesses them with righteousness.

Are they only waiting for the Hour to take them by surprise? Yet ňĻsome ofňļ its signs have already come. <sup foot_note=1042>1042</sup> Once it actually befalls them, will it not be too late to be mindful?

foot_notes.1042: The coming of MuŠł•ammad ( Ô∑ļ ) is one of the signs of the Hour.

text: So, know ňĻwell, O Prophet,ňļ that there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Allah. And seek forgiveness for your shortcomings <sup foot_note=1043>1043</sup> and for ňĻthe sins ofňļ the believing men and women. For Allah ňĻfullyňļ knows your movements and places of rest ňĻO peopleňļ.

foot_notes.1043: Like other prophets, MuŠł•ammad ( Ô∑ļ ) was infallible of sin. The verse here refers to misjudgments, such as the example given in 80:1-10. If the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) himself is urged to seek forgiveness, then the believers are even more in need of praying for Allah‚Äôs forgiveness.

text: And the believers say, ‚ÄúIf only a s√Ľrah was revealed ňĻallowing self-defenceňļ!‚ÄĚ Yet when a precise s√Ľrah is revealed, in which fighting is ňĻexplicitlyňļ mentioned, you see those with sickness in their hearts staring at you like someone in the throes of death. It would have been better for them
to obey and speak rightly. Then when fighting was ordained, it surely would have been better for them if they were true to Allah.
Now if you ňĻhypocritesňļ turn away, perhaps you would then spread corruption throughout the land and sever your ňĻties ofňļ kinship! <sup foot_note=1044>1044</sup>
These are the ones who Allah has condemned, deafening them and blinding their eyes.
Do they not then reflect on the Quran? Or are there locks upon their hearts?
Indeed, those who relapse ňĻinto disbeliefňļ after ňĻtrueňļ guidance has become clear to them, ňĻit isňļ Satan ňĻthatňļ has tempted them, luring them with false hopes.

That is because they said ňĻprivatelyňļ to those who ňĻalsoňļ detest what Allah has revealed, ‚ÄúWe will obey you in some matters.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=1045>1045</sup> But Allah ňĻfullyňļ knows what they are hiding.

foot_notes.1045: i.e., your opposition to the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) .

text: Then how ňĻhorribleňļ will it be when the angels take their souls, beating their faces and backs!
This is because they follow whatever displeases Allah and hate whatever pleases Him, so He has rendered their deeds void.
Or do those with sickness in their hearts think that Allah will not ňĻbe able toňļ expose their malice?

Had We willed, We could have truly shown them to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, and you would have certainly recognized them by their appearance. But you will surely recognize them by their tone of speech. And Allah ňĻfullyňļ knows your doings ňĻO peopleňļ.
We will certainly test you ňĻbelieversňļ until We prove those of you who ňĻtrulyňļ struggle ňĻin Allah‚Äôs causeňļ and remain steadfast, and reveal how you conduct yourselves.

Indeed, those who disbelieve, hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah, and defy the Messenger after ňĻtrueňļ guidance has become clear to them; they will not harm Allah in the least, but He will render their deeds void.
O believers! Obey Allah and obey the Messenger, and do not let your deeds be in vain.
Surely those who disbelieve, hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah, and then die as disbelievers; Allah will never forgive them.
So do not falter or cry for peace, for you will have the upper hand and Allah is with you. And He will never let your deeds go to waste.
This worldly life is no more than play and amusement. But if you are faithful and mindful ňĻof Allahňļ, He will grant you your ňĻfullňļ reward, and will not ask you ňĻto donate allňļ your wealth.
If He were to do so and pressure you, you would withhold and He would bring out your resentment.

Here you are, being invited to donate ňĻa littleňļ in the cause of Allah. Still some of you withhold. And whoever does so, it is only to their own loss. For Allah is the Self-Sufficient, whereas you stand in need ňĻof Himňļ. If you ňĻstillňļ turn away, He will replace you with another people. And they will not be like you.
